name: Pork Collioure Stew
recipeIngredient: Trimmed of fat, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 medium Onion, peeled and roughly chopped
3 medium Parsnips, peeled and cut into 1-inch rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Pat pork dry with paper towels and sprinkle with desired salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat on top of the stove.
4. Add pork, without crowding, and brown well on all sides. You may have to perform this operation in batches.
5. Remove pieces to a plate as they are brown.
6. Pour off all but about 2 tablespoons fat.
7. Reduce heat to low and replace the pot on the stove.
8. Add onion and cook for 5 minutes, scraping up any brown bits that cling to the pan.
9. Add flour and cook another minute, stirring.
10. Add wine, vinegar, and broth and bring to a boil.
11. Return pork to the pot with any juices on the plate and add bay leaves and thyme.
12. Cover tightly and transfer the pot to the oven.
13. Cook for 1 1/4 hours, or until meat is barely tender.
14. Remove casserole from the oven and, using a slotted spoon, remove meat from sauce and set aside.
15. Strain sauce through a fine sieve into a container and discard onions and herbs.
16. Replace meat in the pot, add horseradish, mustard, cream, lemon juice, and parsnips.
17. Cover and replace in oven for 20-to-30 minutes or until meat is tender and parsnips are cooked.
18. Remove from oven and serve with boiled potatoes or buttered noodles.
